title: Hazard-parameterized time-to-stop reformulation
summary: This target is naturally a discrete time-to-event problem: each row asks whether the event happens on the next lap.
rationale: This target is naturally a discrete time-to-event problem: each row asks whether the event happens on the next lap. Survival literature reports that discrete hazard parameterization is often slightly stronger than PMF-style formulations and handles censoring cleanly.
implementation_hint: Create a side model over driver-race sequences that predicts hazard for stop in 1,2,...,H laps using discrete bins until next observed pit or sequence end; use hazard formulation rather than PMF, then extract the 1-lap hazard as the competition prediction and optionally feed longer-horizon hazards back into the main tabular model as features.
expected_effect: May capture consistency across adjacent laps and reduce noisy one-step decisions around ambiguous windows.
risk: This is more engineering-heavy and could miss if most of the lift already comes from simple next-lap signals; sequence construction must be strictly causal.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
